content: Oqto mini-app SDK lives in frontend/mini-apps/: theming/ (base24 scheme -> semantic CSS var mapping via mapSchemeToTokens+applyScheme, oqto-dark/light pinned schemes), sdk/ (OqtoHost contract + defineOqtoApp + createMockHost), shell/ (OqtoAppShell applies scheme + injects host), workbench/ (2nd Vite entry at /workbench.html). Apps run standalone with a mock host, no backend. base24 swaps reskin the whole app. Integration phase (real host, app-registry wiring, a2ui removal) is trx oqto-trdk.5.

Oqto mini-app SDK now has 2 apps in frontend/mini-apps/: photo-editor (PixiJS ColorMatrixFilter adjustments+crop) and photo-grid (resizable collage: draggable row/col dividers via fr units, per-tile cover-crop with object-position pan, batch pool via host.files.pickMultiple, canvas compositor export in lib/cover.ts). OqtoHost gained pickMultiple. Workbench shows a launcher when >1 app. DOM/CSS used for grid (cover-fit maps to auto-crop).

oqto-sandbox build_bwrap_args_for_user seeds the sandbox PATH with canonical system bin dirs (/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin) plus user toolchain dirs (~/.cargo/bin, ~/go/bin, ~/.local/bin, ~/.bun/bin, ~/.npm-global/bin), appended to the inherited PATH if missing+existing. This guarantees bare command resolution (sed, gitleaks) inside the sandbox even when launched from a sparse-PATH context (systemd/cron). It bind-mounts /usr,/bin,/sbin so absolute paths always work; PATH seeding is what makes bare names work too.

oqto-sandbox seccomp policy is a hand-curated allowlist at backend/crates/oqto/examples/seccomp/default.policy.toml (default_action=errno/EPERM). It compiles to default-{arch}.bpf via scripts/sandbox/generate-seccomp-artifacts.sh (cargo run -p oqto-sandbox --bin seccomp-policy-gen) and deploys to /usr/local/share/oqto/seccomp/default.bpf (deploy preflight checks /etc/oqto/seccomp/default.bpf). config.rs treats SeccompMode::Audit and Enforce IDENTICALLY (both --seccomp 198) and there is no SCMP_ACT_LOG artifact, so 'audit' seccomp silently enforces. To debug syscall denials inside the sandbox: probe via python ctypes libc.syscall(nr,...) - blocked syscalls return EPERM(1), allowed ones return EFAULT/EINVAL. Validate policy syscall names against libseccomp with seccomp_syscall_resolve_name before regen (generator hard-bails on unknown names).

deploy.sh now installs the seccomp BPF as part of transactional activation (was preflight-check-only). prepare_host stages both default-{x86_64,aarch64}.bpf into the release dir under seccomp/; activate_host and rollback_host call install_seccomp_bpf() which picks the host arch (uname -m), installs to /usr/local/share/oqto/seccomp/default.bpf + symlinks /etc/oqto/seccomp/default.bpf. Preflight now gates on SOURCE artifact availability (ROOT_DIR/backend/crates/oqto/examples/seccomp/default-<arch>.bpf) not target pre-existence, avoiding first-deploy deadlock. set -euo pipefail means a failed install aborts activation (fail-closed). Standalone scripts/sandbox/install-seccomp-policy.sh still exists for manual installs and does the same thing.

Egress/secret mediation reality check (2026-06): NetworkMode::Proxy is config-only (never consumed in spawn/sandbox.rs), oqto-ssh-proxy binary is orphaned, no kyz integration exists in backend despite closed sandbox-v2 epic oqto-6er8. Live network posture is binary Open/Isolated. Only implemented secret mediation is eavs LLM virtual keys. Target architecture + microvm tier decisions in docs/active/design/20260609-isolation-tiers-and-egress.md (issues oqto-pgwp/h6hr/0q7x/8v0y/430k).
